data.title: Bitcoin Developers Explore Quantum Defense Amid Google Research Findings
data.description: Bitcoin developers are evaluating proposals for quantum defense following Google's recent research indicating that a sufficiently powerful quantum computer could potentially break Bitcoin's core cryptography in less than nine minutes. According to NS3.AI, the article discusses several initiatives, including BIP 360, post-quantum signature schemes, a commit/reveal design for mempool protection, and Hourglass V2. However, it is noted that none of these proposals have been activated yet.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts High Probability of U.S. Military Entering Iran by April 30
data.description:
The probability of U.S. military forces entering Iran by April 30 has surged to 96.5% on Polymarket, marking an 11% increase within 24 hours. According to Odaily, this spike reflects heightened speculation in the prediction market.

An American government official informed Al Jazeera that the second crew member from a downed F-15E fighter jet has been rescued, although the rescue operation is still ongoing. The rescued crew member is confirmed safe but not yet out of danger, as the rescue team must successfully evacuate from Iran and return safely.

Polymarket's rules state that the market will be deemed "yes" if active U.S. military personnel enter Iranian land territory by the specified date (Eastern Time). Special operations forces are included, but intelligence personnel are not. Entry into Iranian waters or airspace does not count.

data.title: The probability of "US troops entering Iran as of April 30" on Polymarket has increased significantly, currently standing at 96.5%.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows a significant increase in the probability of "US military presence in Iran as of April 30" on Polymarket, currently standing at 96.5%. According to Al Jazeera, a US government official told Al Jazeera that the second crew member of the downed F-15E fighter jet has been rescued, and the rescue operation is ongoing. The missing crew member has been confirmed rescued but is not yet out of danger. They added that the rescue team still needs to successfully evacuate from Iran and return safely. According to Polymarket's criteria, if active-duty US military personnel actually entered Iran at any time before the specified date (Eastern Time), the market will rate it "Yes." Otherwise, the market will rate it "No." Special operations forces personnel qualify; however, intelligence personnel are not included. US military personnel must actually enter Iranian land territory to qualify. Entry into Iranian waters or airspace is not counted. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: The second crew member of the downed F-15E fighter jet has been rescued.
data.description: According to Al Jazeera: A U.S. government official said the second crew member of the downed F-15E fighter jet has been rescued after a “fierce firefight.” (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Government Confirms Rescue of Second Crew Member from Downed F-15E Fighter Jet
data.description: A U.S. government official announced the successful rescue of the second crew member from a downed F-15E fighter jet following intense combat. According to BlockBeats, the incident occurred on April 5, as reported by Al Jazeera. The rescue operation was carried out after the aircraft was shot down during a period of heavy fighting. Further details about the circumstances surrounding the incident and the condition of the rescued crew member have not been disclosed.

data.title: U.S. and Iran Engage in Indirect Talks Amid Tensions
data.description:
In recent developments, the United States and Iran have been involved in indirect negotiations through Pakistan, Egypt, and Turkey over the past ten days, aiming to reach a ceasefire agreement contingent on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. According to BlockBeats, these discussions have yet to yield any substantial progress.

The indirect talks are led by U.S. Vice President Vance and Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf, with Pakistan's military chief, Marshal Asim Munir, mediating. White House envoy Whitkov, along with foreign ministers from Iran, Pakistan, Turkey, and Egypt, are also participating in the negotiations. Two sources involved in the talks have disclosed that mediators are still striving to facilitate a direct meeting between the U.S. and Iran, but efforts have been largely unproductive so far.

One source indicated that Iran has rejected any proposals for a temporary ceasefire, demanding a permanent end to hostilities and clear assurances from the U.S. that it will not launch further attacks. Mediators are currently exploring potential trust-building measures as a pathway to direct U.S.-Iran negotiations, though it remains uncertain whether any progress will be made before the Monday deadline.

data.title: IEA Chief Warns of Oil Supply Risks Amid Hormuz Strait Closure
data.description:
The International Energy Agency (IEA) Chief, Fatih Birol, has issued a warning regarding the potential doubling of oil and refined product losses in April if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed to shipping. According to Odaily, Birol emphasized that even if the conflict ends, it will take a significant amount of time to return to normalcy.

Birol stated that the IEA is closely monitoring all critical energy assets in the region, including oil and gas fields, pipelines, refineries, and liquefied natural gas terminals. He noted that 72 energy assets have been damaged, with one-third suffering severe or very severe damage.

He commended Saudi Arabia for its swift response to the crisis, highlighting the country's rerouting of over two-thirds of its oil exports through a pipeline to the Red Sea. Birol mentioned that Saudi authorities assured him of the pipeline's robust protection. However, he cautioned that any attack on this route could have extremely serious consequences for the global economy.

data.title: International Energy Agency Executive Director: Continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz will double losses on crude oil and refined products in April.
data.description: International Energy Agency (IEA) Executive Director Fatih Birol warned that if the Strait of Hormuz does not reopen to shipping, the amount of crude oil and refined products lost in April will be double the amount lost in March. Even after the conflict ends, it will take a long time to return to normal. “We are monitoring all critical energy assets in the region hourly,” he said, referring to oil and gas fields, pipelines, refineries, and liquefied natural gas terminals. “Currently, 72 energy assets have been damaged, and one-third of them are severely or very severely damaged,” he added. Birol praised Saudi Arabia’s swift response to the crisis, noting that the country diverted more than two-thirds of its oil exports via a pipeline to the Red Sea. Birol said that Saudi Arabia’s highest authorities assured him that the critical pipeline was well protected. However, Birol pointed out that if this route were attacked, the consequences for the global economy would be extremely severe. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin Spot ETF Sees Inflows While Ethereum Spot ETF Experiences Outflows
data.description: This week, U.S. Bitcoin spot ETFs recorded a net inflow of $22.2 million, according to BlockBeats. In contrast, Ethereum spot ETFs experienced a net outflow of $42.1 million, as reported by Farside monitoring data.

data.title: UConn Advances to March Madness Finals with Increased Championship Odds
data.description: UConn secured a spot in the March Madness finals by defeating Illinois 71-62. According to NS3.AI, Polymarket has adjusted UConn's championship probability to 32%, marking an 18% increase over the past 24 hours. In another game, Michigan led Arizona with a score of 48-32. Polymarket's data indicates Michigan's probability at 62%, while Arizona stands at 6%.

data.title: The University of Connecticut has reached the Third Madness finals for the 7th time: Polymarket's chances of winning have risen to 32%, an increase of 18% in 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the NCAA Final Four began today, with the University of Connecticut defeating the University of Illinois 71-62 to advance to the March Madness Finals. This marks their 7th appearance in the March Madness Finals, and they have reached it 3 times in the past 4 years. ESPN data shows they have a 6-0 record in their previous 6 Finals appearances, winning all 6. Following this game, Connecticut's championship probability on Polymarket surged to 32%, a 18% increase in 24 hours. The University of Arizona vs. University of Michigan game is currently underway, with Michigan leading 48-32. Michigan's championship probability on Polymarket briefly rose to 62%, a 28% increase in one hour; while Arizona's probability plummeted to 6%, a 30% drop in one hour. NCAA March Madness refers to the national championship of the NCAA Division I men's basketball league. The tournament typically begins in March each year, employing a single-elimination format. Its intensive schedule and intense competition have earned it the nickname "March Madness." Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Drift Identifies Threat Actor Behind Recent Attack
data.description: Drift has released the latest findings on a recent attack, revealing that the same threat actor responsible for the October 2024 Radiant Capital hack carried out the operation. According to PANews, the on-chain fund flow and operational methods closely match those of the previous incident. Mandiant attributes the Radiant Capital hack to UNC4736, an organization linked to the North Korean government. Additionally, the attack was meticulously planned over six months. Starting in the fall of 2025, individuals posing as a 'quantitative trading company' approached Drift contributors at various international crypto conferences.

data.title: Public Companies Shift Focus from Passive Digital Asset Accumulation
data.description: Greengage has highlighted a significant shift in the approach of public companies towards digital asset management. According to NS3.AI, while over 200 public companies held more than $115 billion in digital assets by early 2026, Greengage argues that merely accumulating these assets passively is no longer sufficient. The sector is increasingly moving towards strategies that include staking, generating income through trading, and deploying credit backed by stablecoin liquidity. This shift reflects a broader trend in the industry as companies seek more active and diversified methods to leverage their digital asset holdings.

data.title: Drift: The April 1st attack was part of a long-term infiltration operation planned by a North Korean hacking group.
data.description: Drift Protocol announced on the X platform that its preliminary investigation into the April 1, 2026 attack indicates that the operation was orchestrated by the North Korean government-backed hacking group UNC4736 (also known as AppleJeus or Citrine Sleet). From the fall of 2025, this group engaged in face-to-face interactions with Drift contributors for six months, using methods such as sending intermediaries to crypto conferences and establishing fake quantitative trading companies, to induce them to download malicious code libraries or applications. Drift has currently frozen all protocol functions and removed compromised wallets from multi-signature systems. Mandiant has been invited to participate in an in-depth forensic investigation. The investigation confirms that the on-chain fund flows used to test the operation can be traced back to the Radiant Capital attackers in October 2024.

data.title: Market news: Preliminary investigations into the Drift hack indicate that team members were contacted by a North Korean intermediary during a meeting.
data.description: Market sources indicate that preliminary investigations into the Drift hack suggest that team members were contacted by a North Korean intermediary during a meeting.

data.title: U.S. Senate Banking Committee to Hold Hearing on Fed Chair Nomination
data.description: The U.S. Senate Banking Committee is set to conduct a hearing on April 16 to review the nomination of Kevin Warsh for the position of Federal Reserve Chair. According to Odaily, a criminal investigation concerning the Federal Reserve is ongoing, examining whether current Chair Jerome Powell provided false statements to Congress regarding costly renovations at the Fed's headquarters. Despite the investigation, the nomination process for Warsh is advancing, potentially leading to a conflict with the Trump administration's parallel initiatives. Committee member Thom Tillis has stated he will not vote to confirm Warsh until the investigation concludes, indicating that U.S. President Donald Trump may face challenges in advancing both processes simultaneously. However, by proceeding with the hearing, Trump appears determined to pursue this goal. The Senate Banking Committee has not yet included the hearing in its public schedule.

data.title: Drift Hack Investigation Reveals North Korean Connection
data.description: Market reports indicate that an initial investigation into the Drift hack has uncovered a connection involving a North Korean intermediary. According to BlockBeats, it was revealed that a team member was approached in person by this intermediary during a meeting.

data.title: AI-Powered Banking Malware Attacks Surge 400% in 2025
data.description: AI-powered banking malware attacks have increased by 400% year over year, according to Zimperium's "Mobile Banking Heist" report. The report identified 34 active malware families in 2025, targeting 1,243 financial brands across 90 countries. According to NS3.AI, this significant rise in attacks highlights the growing threat posed by AI-driven cybercrime in the financial sector.

data.description: On Apr 05, 2026, 02:11 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 67,000 USDT and is now trading at 66,995.1875 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.20% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Western Union Acquires Singapore-Based Digital Wallet Company Dash
data.description: Western Union has completed the acquisition of Dash, a digital wallet company based in Singapore. According to BlockBeats, the financial details of the transaction have not been disclosed. Dash was previously owned by Singtel. This acquisition marks Western Union's first digital wallet presence in the Asia-Pacific region and signifies the company's expansion beyond its traditional remittance services into digital financial services.
